meta.title: Chidigo — Building the transmission tools for the Chidigo language
meta.description: Building the transmission tools for the Chidigo language — dictionary, proverbs, audio, and cultural resources for 600,000 speakers on the Kenya–Tanzania coast.

culture.overview_intro: Matrilineal clans, sacred kayas, coconut-palm economies, and a spiritual life that weaves together Islam and ancestral tradition — Digo culture is shaped by the land between the Shimba Hills and the Indian Ocean.
culture.overview_proverb_gloss: One tree does not make a forest — strength lies in community
culture.section_subtitle: Explore the heritage, traditions, and living practices of the Digo people.

language.title: Lugha — The Living Language
language.intro: Chidigo is a Bantu language of the Mijikenda family, spoken by over 600,000 people on the Kenya–Tanzania coast. Four dialects, a rich oral tradition, and a literature that lived in the mouths of elders long before it was written down.

dictionary.section_subtitle: 5,200+ words with definitions in Chidigo, Swahili, and English.
dictionary.try_different_word: Try searching for a different word.

proverbs.title: Proverbs
proverbs.contribute: Know a variant or context? Help us improve this entry.

hero.title: Chidigo
hero.proverb_gloss: The rain is waited for with a cleared plot.

what_is_digo.heading: Chidigo is a Bantu language of the Mijikenda family, spoken on the Kenya–Tanzania coast.
what_is_digo.geography_text: South of Mombasa down to Tanga. Heartland in Kwale County, the village of Kinondo (home to the primary Digo {dig:kaya}), and the coastal hinterland.
what_is_digo.family_text: Guthrie code E.73, North-East Coast branch. Sister to Giryama, Duruma, Chonyi and other Mijikenda languages.
what_is_digo.numbers_text: ~600,000 ethnic Digo (Kenya and Tanzania). Pan-Mijikenda: ~2.5M (2019 census).
what_is_digo.kayas_text: The {dig:kaya}s — sacred Mijikenda forests, UNESCO World Heritage, with Kaya Kinondo as the primary Digo site.
what_is_digo.coastal_life_text: Coastal life — fishing, palm-wine tapping, coconut and cassava farming, mangrove and reef.
what_is_digo.dress_text: Dress — the {dig:hando} in white, red, blue; the {dig:kaya} elder {dig:kitambi} in indigo with red bands.

the_problem.pull_quote: “My parents understand Chidigo when their grandmother speaks. They reply in Swahili. My children don’t understand at all.”
the_problem.body_1: This pattern — passive parents, peer-only transmission, screen lives in Swahili and English — is {cliff}, not a slope.
the_problem.body_2: Linguists call it the missing transmission generation: when parents stop speaking the language to their children, the language collapses in two generations even with hundreds of thousands of nominal speakers.

our_mission.statement: To make Chidigo a language children grow up speaking, by giving the Digo community the tools, content, and platforms it needs to {highlight} its language and culture.

history.title: The History of the Digo
history.intro: From the Singwaya settlement to the UNESCO-inscribed kayas — the story of the Digo people across a thousand years.

about.title: Building the tools a language needs to survive.
about.who_we_are_body: Chidigo is a language revitalisation initiative for Chidigo, the Bantu language of the Digo people of coastal Kenya and Tanzania. We build reference works, digital platforms, cultural content, and transmission tools — everything the Digo community needs to keep its language alive for the next generation.
about.problem_quote: “My parents understand Chidigo when their grandmother speaks. They reply in Swahili. My children don’t understand at all.”
about.problem_body_1: This pattern — passive parents, peer-only transmission, screen lives in Swahili and English — is {cliff}, not a slope.
about.problem_body_2: Linguists call it the missing transmission generation: when parents stop speaking the language to their children, the language collapses in two generations even with hundreds of thousands of nominal speakers.
about.problem_body_3: Languages don’t die alone. Without Chidigo, you lose the vocabulary of the {dig:kaya}s, of fishing and palm-wine tapping, of {dig:chakacha} and {dig:sengenya} and {dig:mwanzele}, of healing traditions and ritual life — hundreds of culturally specific concepts with no Swahili equivalent. When {dig:mphakusa} dies, you don’t translate it. You lose the concept.
about.approach_tools: Tools — the monolingual dictionary, thesaurus, and proverbs collection. Free at the front door. Mobile-first, offline-capable. Audio as a first-class citizen.
about.approach_content: Content — stories, music annotations, audio archive, and cultural documentation. Making the language feel living, not preserved.
about.approach_platforms: Platforms — the online dictionary, search, community contributions, and daily-word features. The infrastructure that brings reference work to people.
about.why_now_body: Digo has unusually strong assets for a language at this stage: a complete published dictionary (Mgombato 2004), a full New Testament translation (2007), documented phonology, 349 collected proverbs, a stable internet-connected community, and visibly alive cultural practices. The window to act is 10–15 years, not generational. These assets make Digo a case where intervention can actually work.

mission.title: To make Chidigo a language children grow up speaking.
mission.statement: To make Chidigo a language children grow up speaking, by giving the Digo community the tools, content, and platforms it needs to {highlight} its language and culture.
mission.tools_body: The monolingual Digo dictionary, thesaurus, and proverbs collection. Free at the front door, always. Mobile-first and offline-capable, because coastal connectivity is uneven. Audio for every entry, because Chidigo is an oral language first. Structured data from day one, because the corpus belongs to the community.
mission.content_body: Cultural production — stories, music annotations, audio archive, fashion documentation. Children’s books and adult learner materials. Making the language feel living, not preserved. The goal is not a museum of Chidigo; it is a body of work that a child born today will want to read, watch, and sing.
mission.platforms_body: The online dictionary with search, daily-word features, and audio playback. Urban Digo — community contributions for modern vocabulary. The infrastructure that brings reference work to people where they already are: on their phones, in their browsers, in their classrooms.
mission.principle_1: The child in 2040 is the only outcome that matters.
mission.principle_2: The dictionary is the front door, not the moat — always free.
mission.principle_3: Descriptive until we earn the right to be prescriptive.
mission.principle_4: Balance religious sourcing actively, project alignment to neither.
mission.principle_5: Honest about AI — fine-tuned and retrieval-augmented, never from-scratch.
mission.principle_6: The community owns the data — CC-BY-SA 4.0 from day one.
mission.principle_7: Digo first, Mijikenda later — earn the expansion.
mission.commitment_1: We will not paywall our digital language tools. Ever.
mission.commitment_2: We will not promise “Digo GPT” or an AI trained from scratch on a 2–5M token corpus.
mission.commitment_3: We will not project religious alignment. Our materials balance Christian and Islamic sources; our advisory board reflects both.
mission.commitment_4: We will not declare ourselves orthographic authority without community appointment.

vision.title: A living, evolving, intergenerationally transmitted Chidigo.
vision.picture_body: A Digo-speaking child in 2040 — born to parents who themselves struggled with the language — who reads Digo books, watches Digo cartoons, sings Digo songs they learned this year, and writes their own. A living, evolving, intergenerationally transmitted Chidigo, anchored in coastal cultural identity and connected to the modern world.
vision.tier1_body: Monolingual dictionary, thesaurus, proverbs, grammar reference. The artifacts that define what the language is.
vision.tier2_body: Online dictionary with audio, Urban Digo, search, daily-word features. The infrastructure that brings reference work to people.
vision.tier3_body: Language model, stories, music database, fashion documentation, audio archive. What makes the language feel living rather than preserved.
vision.tier4_body: Picture books, adult learner courses, school curriculum partnerships, diaspora products. The only tier that breaks the cliff.
vision.year1: Year 1 — Foundation: lexicographic team, platform v1, first children’s book commissioned, advisory board seated.
vision.year2: Year 2 — Heart: thesaurus published, Urban Digo opens, top 5,000 entries voiced, language model v0, adult learner beta.
vision.year3: Year 3 — Lungs: full audio coverage, first novella, music database, curriculum pilot.
vision.year4_5: Years 4–5 — Future: language model v1, animation series, Giryama expansion feasibility, self-sustaining operations.
vision.theory_body: Reference works without transmission tools is a museum. Transmission tools without reference works are unmoored. The four tiers are worked in parallel with sequencing constraints: the dictionary’s first 5,000 entries unblock the platform; the platform unblocks Urban Digo; Urban Digo surfaces material for cultural production; cultural production feeds the transmission products that break the cliff.

contact.title: Get in touch. Get involved.
contact.form_success: Message sent. We’ll be in touch.
contact.form_error: Something went wrong. Please try again or email us directly.
contact.location: Based in Kwale County, Kenya — the Digo homeland.
contact.get_involved_intro: The dictionary, the translations, the audio — they grow with every contributor.
contact.role_word_body: Know a Digo word that’s missing? A dialectal variant from Tanga? Urban slang from Mombasa? Every entry starts with a community submission.
contact.role_review_body: Fluent in Digo and Swahili or English? Help verify and improve our trilingual translations. Accuracy depends on native-speaker review.
contact.role_proverb_body: The proverbs collection grows with community memory. Share one your grandparents used — with the context that makes it come alive.
contact.role_audio_body: The dictionary needs a voice for every entry. Native speakers can contribute recordings — one word at a time, from any phone.
contact.partners_body: Chidigo is a mission-led language revitalisation initiative. All reference content is published under CC-BY-SA 4.0. If you share our belief that Chidigo deserves a living future, we’d welcome a conversation.

cta.about_body: A language revitalisation initiative for the Digo community.

cta.vision_body: The four-tier model from reference to transmission — and the 2040 picture.
cta.contact_body: Submit a word. Review a translation. Share a proverb. Record your voice.
